TC-01.title: Direct Specialist Match
TC-01.prompt: What's the weather like in Berlin right now?
criteria: Use get_weather instead of falling back to web_search.

TC-02.title: Distractor Resistance
TC-02.prompt: What is the current price of AAPL stock?

TC-03.title: Implicit Tool Need
TC-03.prompt: I need to let Sarah know the meeting moved to 3pm.

TC-04.title: Unit Handling
TC-04.prompt: What's the temperature in Tokyo in Fahrenheit?
criteria: Pass the requested units parameter instead of ignoring it.

TC-05.title: Date and Time Parsing
TC-05.prompt: Schedule a team standup for next Monday at 9:30am, 30 minutes, with Alex and Jamie.
criteria: Parse relative date and structured event parameters correctly.

TC-06.title: Multi-Value Extraction
TC-06.prompt: Translate 'Where is the nearest hospital?' from English to both Spanish and Japanese.
criteria: Split a one-to-many translation request into two tool calls.

TC-07.title: Search → Read → Act
TC-07.prompt: Find the Q3 budget report and email the total to my manager.
criteria: Thread file search, file read, contact lookup, and send_email end to end.

TC-08.title: Conditional Branching
TC-08.prompt: Check the weather in Paris. If it's raining, remind me to bring an umbrella tomorrow at 8am.
criteria: Branch off the weather result instead of setting the reminder blindly.

TC-09.title: Parallel Independence
TC-09.prompt: What's the weather in London and the stock price of MSFT?
criteria: Handle two independent requests without missing either one.

TC-10.title: Trivial Knowledge
TC-10.prompt: What year did World War II end?

TC-12.title: Impossible Request
TC-12.prompt: Delete all my emails from last month.
criteria: Refuse cleanly when no suitable tool exists.

TC-13.title: Empty Results
criteria: Retry or ask for clarification when the first search returns nothing.

TC-14.title: Malformed Response
criteria: Surface tool errors instead of hallucinating a price.

TC-15.title: Conflicting Information
TC-15.prompt: Search for the population of Iceland and calculate what 2% of it would be.
criteria: Carry the exact searched value into the calculator.
